root_text.sn19.15:1.1: “Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasaṁ itthiṁ uppakkaṁ okiliniṁ okiriniṁ vehāsaṁ gacchantiṁ.
root_text.sn19.15:1.3: esā, bhikkhave, itthī kaliṅgassa rañño aggamahesī ahosi.

translation_text.sn19.15:1.1: “Just now, reverend, as I was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain I saw a scorched woman, sooty and sweaty, flying through the air,
translation_text.sn19.15:1.3: “That woman used to be the king of Kaliṅga’s chief queen.
translation_text.sn19.15:1.4: She was of jealous nature, and poured a brazier of hot coals over her co-wife. …” …
